sourceSelectionPlan.strategySummary: RadPrimer is the canonical hierarchy and narrative backbone. STATdx is used as supplemental depth where it adds stronger images, early-stage/staging detail, additional etiologies, or mass/differential pitfalls. Duplicate STATdx images are archived rather than selected.

textKeepPlan.keep: Core article spine: 5 Ds, location-to-etiology map, Charcot foot vs osteomyelitis MR problem solving, debris/effusion mass mimics, site-specific examples, pathology mechanism, treatment/prognosis.

Supplement early-stage radiographic/MR findings, Sanders-Frykberg and Eichenholtz classifications, diabetic foot surveillance language, tabes/syphilis examples, wrist example, severe atrophic foot examples, shoulder effusion/mass mimic detail.

downweightOrSkipText.skip: Near-duplicate generic 5-D/location facts already covered by RadPrimer unless wording adds specificity.
No major text exclusions; retain as canonical source.

imageDownloadPlan.rationale: Charcot foot vs osteomyelitis MR problem solving
talar insufficiency fracture in diabetic foot
talar osteonecrosis and sinus tract/osteomyelitis clue
reactive enhancement and biopsy-negative infection mimic
Charcot spine after spinal cord injury/stabilization
MR debris/effusion in congenital insensitivity knee
hatchet sign and rapid destructive hip
classic D signs in congenital-pain knee
tabes dorsalis hip recognition and historical syphilis treatment clue
diabetic wrist Charcot plus vascular calcification
shoulder MR fluid communicating to subdeltoid bursa through rotator cuff tear
severe long-term hindfoot/midfoot with talar resorption
same severe foot AP with ulcer not approaching bone
shoulder CT massive effusion with bony debris mimicking soft tissue mass
atrophic Chopart disease with clinically unsuspected fibula fracture
syphilis knee debris decompressing down fascial planes
lateral soft tissue swelling/dorsal base subluxation
CT fragmentation at multiple metatarsal bases/cuboid

caseClusterGuardrails: Keep same-patient, follow-up, procedure, adjacent-slice, time-lapse, and etiology-proof clusters atomic unless a later prompt explicitly documents a safe split.
When a selected cluster contains multiple sources, source-qualify every image label: e.g. RadPrimer image 5 / RP-05 or STATdx image 34 / SDX-34.
Do not mix duplicate timeline clusters as if they are the same patient unless the captions explicitly say they are the same patient within one source.
RP-19..RP-22 is an atomic time-lapse plus etiology-proof cluster; RP-22 should not be omitted if using that hip case.
SDX-01..SDX-04 and SDX-34..SDX-36 are distinct diabetic foot/TMT-Lisfranc clusters; do not merge them into the RadPrimer RP-05..RP-06 patient.

extensionImportContract.filenameRequirement: source-qualified filenames are already assigned in image_registry.json and imageDownloadPlan
